message:
fix: per-pane memory leak, division-by-zero, sixel overflow, lock poisoning

1. sessionhandler.rs + dispatch.rs: per_pane HashMap entries were never
   removed when panes were killed or received PaneRemoved notifications.
   Long-lived sessions would accumulate stale entries without bound.
   Added remove_per_pane() method and call it on KillPane and
   PaneRemoved. Also fixed bare .unwrap() on lock in dispatch.rs
   Alert handler (inconsistent with sessionhandler which uses
   .map_err()).

2. GUI division-by-zero (4 sites): mouseevent.rs, tab_bar.rs, mod.rs
   each divided by cell_size.width/height without guarding against
   zero. Previous fix only covered resize.rs. Added .max(1) guards to
   all remaining division-by-cell-size sites.

3. charselect.rs + palette.rs: Same division-by-zero, plus a usize
   underflow: `(height / cell_h) - 2` panics when result < 2. Changed
   to .saturating_sub(2).

4. sixel.rs: x-coordinate increments used bare += 1 while y already
   used saturating_add(6). Changed to saturating_add(1) for
   consistency, preventing u32 wrapping on malicious sixel input.

Validated: 34 mux-server-impl tests, 261 term tests,
GUI + all modified crates compile clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -63,7 +63,7 @@ impl crate::TermWindow {
                 palette: &palette,
                 dims: &RenderableDimensions {
                     cols: self.dimensions.pixel_width
-                        / self.render_metrics.cell_size.width as usize,
+                        / (self.render_metrics.cell_size.width as usize).max(1),
                     physical_top: 0,
                     scrollback_rows: 0,
                     scrollback_top: 0,
